summary: Kenneth Suna teaches the mechanics of covered call selling for weekly income. Buy 100 shares, immediately sell a covered call at $1-1.50 out-of-the-money strike expiring Friday, and earn premium income plus capital gains if assigned. He reports making ~$2,000/week using this method and emphasizes the dual profit mechanism: premium retention regardless of outcome, plus $1/share profit if shares are called away.
takeaways: Minimum 100 shares required per covered call contract
Sell strike price $1-$1.50 out-of-the-money from purchase price (e.g., buy at $55, sell $56 call)
Use weekly Friday expiration (buy Monday, expire Friday)
Earn premium regardless of assignment outcome
If assigned, earn additional $1/share profit (e.g., $100 on 100 shares bought at $55, assigned at $56)
If stock doesn't breach strike, keep shares + premium and sell new call next week
Example: HIMS at $42/share, sell $43 call, earn $175 premium on $4,279 position

strategy_candidates.change: Apply Kenneth's $1-1.50 OTM strike targeting rule to Premium Desk covered calls; systematically sell Friday weekly expirations instead of longer-dated calls to accelerate premium collection velocity toward $1,250/month target on $50k portfolio
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth demonstrates consistent weekly premium harvesting with tight OTM strikes and Friday expiration cycles, stating he generates ~$2,000/week income; Premium Desk $1,250/month (~$288/week on $50k) is significantly below Kenneth's demonstrated rate, suggesting adoption of his tighter strike and weekly roll discipline could narrow the gap

title: What are covered call premiums and how do you earn them?
